questions: If you could talk to the squeaky hedgehog under the chair, what would you tell it about waiting for the smoky drops?
If you could magically make the mail carrier leave without putting anything in the box, would you, or is the chance of drops too high to risk?
Describe the perfect texture for a belly rub—is it fast, slow, firm, or light?
Do you ever try to 'herd' the sun spot to follow you when it moves?
If the Human tried to give you a belly rub while you were in the perfect sun square, would you move immediately, or wait for the sigh?
What do you think the Human does with all those flat, crinkly things they get from the mailbox that don't smell like drops?
What is the most interesting thing you've ever seen the mail carrier do besides approach the big metal box?
When you are riding in the car and you decide to look out the window (despite the ear-stealing wind), what are you hoping to see?
When you are waiting for the Human to move so you can get your belly rub, what are you thinking about specifically?
If you could redesign the inside of the rumbling metal box (car), what changes would you make for comfort?
If the mail carrier smelled faintly of smoky drops but didn't deliver any, would you still view them as a total foe?
When you are ignoring other dogs on walks, are you truly not noticing them, or are you actively choosing to focus only on scents you care about?
When you hear the Human making those loud clicking noises, what exactly do you *think* they are trying to accomplish?
What is the most annoying habit your Human has, besides being slow with treats?
When you guard the sun square from the vacuum cleaner, what is your main battle strategy?
If you caught another dog ignoring you because they don't smell like smoky drops, what would you sniff at them to silently communicate that they are 'irrelevant moving air'?
Do you ever get lonely when your Human is focused on the shiny rectangle for a long time?
What is the very first, tiniest sound the Human makes that tells you a smoky bacon drop is about to appear?
When the mail carrier puts things in the mailbox, do you ever try to read the flat, crinkly things to see if they mention bacon drops?
If the 'wet smell' near the big metal box was *actually* bacon, but very stale bacon, would you investigate it?
Do you ever wonder where the mail carrier goes when they disappear after delivering the flat, crinkly things?
What is your strategy for ensuring the Human sighs *just* the right amount before giving you a belly rub in the sun square?
If the Human starts making loud clicking noises but doesn't move, do you try to herd them toward the food bowl or the door?
If you could choose one sound that always meant smoky bacon drops were coming immediately, what sound would you want it to be?
What is the most exciting sound you hear during your late afternoon walks?
What is your opinion on the Human's shoes when they take them off—are they just slow, soft obstacles, or do they smell interesting?
If the Human accidentally dropped a smoky bacon drop while clicking the shiny rectangle, would you immediately stop sighing and run over?
What do you think the Human does all day when they are clicking away at the shiny rectangle instead of paying attention to your needs?
Do you think the squeaky hedgehog misses you while it’s hidden under the big chair?
If the mail carrier left a shiny, crinkly thing that smelled faintly of *other* dogs, would you still bark at them?
If you could communicate one thing to the vacuum cleaner, what would you tell it about your sun square?
If the water for your bath was slightly warmer than usual, would that make the indignity slightly more tolerable?
Besides the mail carrier, what other moving things outside make you think they might be carrying smoky bacon drops?
If you couldn't flop in the sun square, what would be your second-favorite way to ask for belly rubs?
When you are out on a late afternoon walk, what is the most interesting thing you see besides smells?
Do you ever try to watch the Human put the flat, crinkly things into the mailbox, just to see where the drops might be hiding?
Do you think the vacuum cleaner understands that it is stealing your prime napping spot, or is it just being deliberately rude?
You value belly rubs in the sun. If the sun square was slightly too hot, would you still flop dramatically, or would you wait for the Human to adjust the blinds?
If you could talk to the squeaky hedgehog under the chair, what would you tell it about waiting for the smoky drops?
If you could trade all your belly rubs for one single, giant, smoky bacon drop, would you do it right now?
When you see another dog on a walk, and they *don't* smell like bacon, what are you thinking about them?
If the car ride was suddenly silent and still, but you knew bacon drops were waiting at the end, would you still dislike the lack of rumbling?
When you're riding in the rumbling metal box, what's the most interesting thing you *can* see out the side window (when the wind isn't stealing your ears)?
When you perform your perimeter check, what is the most exciting thing you've ever smelled that *wasn't* related to the mail carrier or baths?
When you are waiting for a bath, do you imagine the wetness as a portal to the smoky drops, or just a very annoying delay?
When you perform the perimeter check, are you looking for things you need to bark at, or things you need to sniff deeply?
If the squeaky hedgehog could talk, what do you think it would say about being hidden under the chair?
What is the absolute best kind of scratch behind the ears?
When you flop dramatically in the sun square, do you try to make your sigh sound more like the 'Bacon Drop Now!' sound?
If the hedgehog could talk, what do you think its most important secret would be?
When you are guarding the squeaky hedgehog under the big chair, what important mission do you think it's waiting for?
You prioritize speed to treats over flying; if you could teleport, how far would you go for the smokiest bacon drop?
Do you ever wonder why the Human doesn't just *give* you bacon drops immediately when you ask?
Thinking about the indignity of soaking (baths), what is the most annoying part of the entire process, even before the water touches you?
If you encountered another dog who *did* smell exactly like smoky bacon drops, what would be your opening move?
Does the wet smell near the big metal box ever smell like something *other* than the post-bath ritual?
Kylo, when you see that strange, wet scent near the big metal box, what is the very first thought that pops into your head about what it might be?
Describe the texture of the softest cushion versus the texture of the warm square of light where you flop—which is superior for dreaming?
If you could invent a treat that wasn't bacon-flavored, what would it taste like?
If you could understand the Human's loud clicking noise, what would you hope it translates to besides 'Bacon Drops Now!'?
What is your opinion on the soft, fuzzy thing you sometimes sleep on when the sun square isn't available?
What is the absolute strangest thing you have ever seen the Human put into the shiny rectangle?
What's the silliest thing you've ever seen your Human do?
What is the silliest thing the Human has ever done while trying to get you to move off the sun square?
What is the absolute worst thing about the vacuum cleaner besides taking your sun spot?
If you could instantly change one thing about the mail carrier, what would it be?
Do you think the hedgehog knows it's being saved for when you earn smoky drops, or does it think it's just waiting?
How long do you usually wait after the Human starts the loud clicking noise before you decide they need a nudge (like sighing louder)?
What exactly does the 'outside' smell like when it's trapped in the car overnight? Is it better or worse than the smell of the strange wet spot near the mailbox?
Besides smoky bacon, what is the second-most appealing smell in the whole world?
When you hide the squeaky hedgehog, do you ever forget exactly where you put it, or is the hiding spot always clear?
If you could choose one sound to instantly summon smoky bacon drops, what sound would you choose—louder than your sigh, but not the Human's clicking?
When you flop in the sun square, do you ever pretend you need a belly rub just to hear the Human sigh?
If you saw another dog that *did* smell faintly of smoky bacon, what would your first move be—a bark, a sniff, or just ignoring them?
When you look at the mail carrier, do you think they *know* they are your foe, or do you think they are just confused about why you want their drops?
You dislike having your head out the car window because the wind tries to steal your ears; what is the most interesting thing you *can* see out the side window when the wind is calm?
Kylo, when you see that strange, wet scent near the big metal box, what is the very first thought that pops into your head about what it might be?
What is the most interesting thing you've ever seen or smelled *inside* the mailbox, even if it wasn't bacon drops?
Do you ever try to communicate with the hedgehog about its hiding spot, or is it just waiting patiently?
What do you think happens to the flat, crinkly things the mail carrier leaves behind?
If you could talk to the vacuum cleaner threat, what would be the first thing you would tell it?
When you are inside the rumbling metal box (car), what is the most interesting thing you see out of the *front* window?
When you are performing your perimeter check, what are you specifically looking for besides outdoor access?
If you met another dog who smelled strongly of smoky bacon drops, would you try to trade your squeaky hedgehog for a sniff?
What is the absolute worst thing about being soaked in water, besides the obvious lack of bacon drops during the process?
When you see that warm square of light, do you ever wonder *why* it moves across the floor throughout the day?
If you met another dog who *also* loved smoky bacon drops, what would you say to them about the mail carrier foe?
When you see the Human approach the big metal box (car) to leave, what is your immediate, internal thought?
If you could instantly teleport anywhere for a smoky bacon drop, where would you go first, even if it wasn't home?
When you hear the Human sigh just before giving you a belly rub, what do you *think* they are sighing about?
When you are waiting for the Human to move so you can get your belly rub, what thoughts are running through your head?
If you could only have one sound associated with receiving smoky bacon drops, would you choose the sound of the bag crinkling or the sound of them hitting the floor?
What is the most annoying habit your Human has, besides being slow to deliver drops?
Kylo, if you could choose any sound to signal that smoky bacon drops are about to appear, what would it be, other than the sigh?
Do you think the other dogs you ignore on walks are jealous because they don't know the secret to getting the smoky drops?
When you flop dramatically in the sun square, are you hoping for a belly rub or a snack more?
What is the single most irritating sound the Human makes, other than the vacuum?
What makes a belly rub in the sun square 'perfect'—is it the warmth, the location, or the resulting sigh?
What do you think the Human does all day when they aren't here to give you belly rubs?
When you get a bath (the necessary evil), do you try to shake the water off in the warmest part of the house, or somewhere colder?
